name: Daniel B Chaires Park and Community Center
description: Daniel B Chaires Park and Community Center is a place to play pickleball in Tallahassee, FL. There are 4 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts cannot be reserved. The courts are free. Amenities include lights.

name: Four Oaks Park
description: Four Oaks Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Tallahassee, FL. There are 6 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include lights, water, and restrooms. The courts are free. Reservations are available.

name: Jack L. McLean Jr. Community Center
description: Jack L. McLean Jr. Community Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Tallahassee, FL. There are 4 hard courts. 2 are indoors and 2 are outdoors. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts can be reserved. Amenities include restrooms. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Jake Gaither Community Center
description: Jake Gaither Community Center is a place to play pickleball in Tallahassee, FL. There are 3 hard courts. 1 is indoors and 2 are outdoors. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. Amenities include restrooms and water. The courts are free.

name: Lawrence Gregory Community Center
description: Come play pickleball at Lawrence Gregory Community Center in Tallahassee, FL! There are 3 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. The facility includes amenities like restrooms. Reservations are available.

name: Lincoln Neighborhood Center

name: Northside Community Center
description: Come play pickleball at Northside Community Center in Tallahassee, FL! There are 3 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Players have access to restrooms. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Premier Health & Fitness Center

A membership is required to play. The facility includes amenities like locker rooms, restrooms, food, lights, and water.

name: Sue Herndon McCollum Community Center
description: Come play pickleball at Sue Herndon McCollum Community Center in Tallahassee, FL! There are 2 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and water. A one-time fee is required to play. You can reserve the courts.

name: Tallahassee Senior Center
description: Tallahassee Senior Center is a place to play pickleball in Tallahassee, FL. There are 2 indoor hard and wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and water. The courts cannot be reserved. The courts are free.

name: The Pickle Pad & Crave Social Eatery
description: The Pickle Pad is not just another gym.  We offer indoor courts with an outdoor experience while staying a constant 72 degrees!  Our permanent cushion x courts provide a slight cushion while playing.  Once you’re finished with your game, grab a bite to eat or a cocktail at our bar and hang out int he yards while playing cornhole, dart, pool and more!  The Pickle Pad is much more than pickleball!
fees: Fee required — Pay by the hour

name: Tom Brown Tennis Center
description: Come play pickleball at Tom Brown Tennis Center in Tallahassee, FL! There are 12 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include restrooms, a pro shop or equipment store, water, wheelchair accessibility, lights, and trainers or lessons. The courts are free.

name: Walker-Ford Community Center
description: Come play pickleball at Walker-Ford Community Center in Tallahassee, FL! There are 5 hard courts. 3 are outdoors and 2 are indoors. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free. Players have access to restrooms, water, and lights. You can’t reserve the courts.

open_play_schedule: There is no “Open Play.” It is reserved or free-for-all.
